title: grastidium clausum
side_effects: Grastidium clausum can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its high concentration of toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to the plant's vapors has also been linked to respiratory issues, including coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath. Improper handling of the plant can also lead to skin irritation and allergic reactions, resulting in redness, itching, and blistering. Furthermore, ingesting even a small amount of the plant can cause cardiac problems, such as arrhythmias and decreased blood pressure.
